title_original: [PAPER] Computing with many encoded logical qubits beyond break-even
summary_original: Researchers at Quantinuum, including Shival Dasu and Matthew DeCross, demonstrated quantum computations using up to 94 logical qubits and up to 48 logical qubits in concatenated codes on the 98-qubit Helios trapped-ion processor. By implementing high-rate iceberg quantum error detection and concatenated quantum error correction codes, and using new techniques for encoded operations, they achieved “beyond break-even” performance—where encoded algorithms outperform unencoded counterparts—on benchmarks like state preparation, logical gate benchmarking, and quantum simulation. The study introduced performance improvements in initialization, syndrome extraction, and gate operations, and found that increasing code distance via concatenation suppresses postselection rates, providing evidence that high-rate codes are viable for near-term, beyond-classical-scale quantum computing.
